prompt: A richly detailed vertical editorial travel illustration of a bustling Seoul market alley rendered in polished hand-drawn realism with warm cinematic lighting, dense urban storytelling, and highly legible signage. The scene is packed with a large crowd of pedestrians, shoppers, diners, street-food customers, musicians, and commuters in a narrow neighborhood market street, viewed from a slightly elevated eye level so the full alley feels layered and alive. At the top left, a large illustrated poster reads “WHERE IS KYLE?” with the subtitle “Can you find Kyle in Seoul?” in bold playful English typography, styled like a search-and-find book cover. The environment should clearly be Seoul, with a visible Namsan tower in the distant skyline, tangled overhead power lines, rooftop seating, stacked low-rise buildings, shop signs in Korean, and a subway entrance sign on the right showing line numbers 4, 2, 4, 4 and the bilingual text “서울역 방면 / To Seoul Station.” Include exactly 15 major visible signs or text panels: 1) the “WHERE IS KYLE?” poster, 2) “할머니 손칼국수”, 3) a hanging vertical sign “낙원상가 2F”, 4) a small blue street sign, 5) a vertical sign “이름 떡볶이”, 6) the large market arch sign “황진다운시장 영”, 7) a vertical orange sign “커피”, 8) a wall mural map with “남산 가는길”, 9) the subway sign with “출입구 4” and “서울역 방면 / To Seoul Station”, 10) the produce sign “경성 야채”, 11) the lower produce sign “신선한 채소”, 12) a handwritten busker sign, 13) the street-food cart sign “호떡·어묵·붕어빵”, 14) the menu board “오늘의 추천 김밥 떡볶이 순대 튀김”, and 15) the delivery box text “빠른 배달”. Populate the alley with exactly 8 vendor or activity zones: 1) a noodle restaurant at left under a red-and-white awning with seated diners, 2) a vegetable stall with stacked produce crates, 3) a busking duo with guitar and percussion, 4) a central street-food cart selling hotteok, fish cake, and bungeoppang, 5) a standing crowd eating snacks and holding cups, 6) a small magic performance table in the lower center with cards and cups, 7) a painter at an easel near the lower left, and 8) a scooter delivery cluster at the lower right. Add exactly 6 notable background accents: 1) rooftop diners on the upper right terrace, 2) a cat lounging on a roof ledge upper left, 3) colorful market umbrellas in the middle distance, 4) stairways and narrow side passages, 5) dense balconies and windows full of plants and people, and 6) the distant tower and skyline. The crowd should feel extremely varied in age and clothing, mostly casual contemporary Korean street fashion in jackets, hoodies, backpacks, skirts, slacks, and sneakers, with a few office workers and students. Hide a single subtly distinct person meant to be the findable target, a young man in a blue hoodie with yellow lettering, integrated naturally among the central crowd rather than spotlighted. Use a vibrant but grounded palette of brick red, teal, forest green, cream, mustard, asphalt gray, and sky blue. Emphasize intricate linework, readable Korean text, observational city details, lively human gestures, and the playful feel of a premium “Where’s Waldo?” style Seoul search poster. Aspect ratio 2:3.
